name: Corn Relish
recipeInstructions:
1. In a kettle large enough to hold the corn, bring water to a boil.
2. Remove the husks and silk from the corn and add the corn, all at one time, to the boiling water.
3. Cook for 5 minutes.
4. Prepare a large bowl of ice water.
5. When the corn is cooked, remove the ears from the boiling water and immediately plunge them into the ice water to stop the cooking.
6. When cool, remove from the water and cut the kernels from the cobs. You should have about 2 quarts of kernels for this recipe.
7. Chop the celery, green and red peppers, and onion into very small pieces.
8. In a large kettle combine the corn kernels, chopped vegetables, spices, water, vinegar, and sugar.
9. Bring to a boil and then reduce heat and cook, uncovered, for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally.
10. If you prefer a thickened sauce, combine 2 tablespoons flour with 1/4 cup water until lump-free (the easiest way is to put flour and water in a small jar, put on a screw-top lid, and shake vigorously). Add the flour-water combination to the boiling relish.
11. Process in a boiling water bath for 15 minutes.
YIELD: about 6 pints
